knowledge.title: Anubis A site: heaven hold with one-way smoke
knowledge.description: CT setup: one player heaven (above A main), one player site behind statue. Heaven uses a one-way smoke at A main entry that lets the heaven player see feet while T entries cannot see up. This single utility setup wins ~70% of A holds against fast hits.
knowledge.situation: CT-side A defense, fast A hit
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: heaven one-way smoke is the single most-used Anubis A defensive setup

knowledge.title: Anubis B site: water entrance trade setup
knowledge.description: CT B setup: one player on platform (close angle), one player water connector (off-angle). Water player traps the second T through B halls, platform player traps the first. Avoid both players on platform — easy double frag from B halls smoke wallbang.
knowledge.situation: CT-side B defense, B halls execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: water-platform crossfire wins ~65% of B defaults

knowledge.title: Anubis mid: window control = round control
knowledge.description: Whoever controls the mid window has line of sight into both bridges and can rotate to either site. CTs should hold window with utility. Ts must smoke window before any mid-to-A or mid-to-B split. Losing window control on Anubis predicts a round loss ~60% of the time.
knowledge.situation: Mid control battle
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: window control predicts round outcome better than any other Anubis stat

knowledge.title: Anubis A execute: smoke connector + window + molly statue
knowledge.description: Standard A execute: smoke connector (cuts mid rotate), smoke window (cuts heaven), molly behind statue (clears default plant spot), flash over palace for entries. Plant default; one player lurks connector for late rotations. ~72% plant rate with full utility.
knowledge.situation: T-side A execute, full buy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: full-utility A execute is the most reliable Anubis T-side strat

knowledge.title: Anubis B execute: smoke heaven + molly platform + flash water
knowledge.description: B execute: smoke heaven (cuts the rotate from A), molly platform (clears the close angle), flash from water for the entry. Plant for the water angle so the lurker can hold from B halls. Avoid plants under platform — too close for retake angles.
knowledge.situation: T-side B execute, full buy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: water-side plant outperforms platform-side plant by ~9 points on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis CT utility: pre-throw molly A main on T spawn
knowledge.description: Pre-throw molly to A main entry within the first 5 seconds. This delays any fast A rush by ~3 seconds and forces Ts to use a flash to push through. Cheap, consistent, and pairs with the heaven hold setup.
knowledge.situation: CT-side A defense, anti-rush
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: pre-throw A main molly is standard CT setup on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis pistol: CTs prefer A stack
knowledge.description: On pistol round Ts often hit A main fast for the open layout. CTs should stack 3 A (heaven, site, connector) and play 2 B passive. Mid is rotational only on pistol — do not commit to mid until rifle rounds.
knowledge.situation: Pistol round, CT setup
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 3A-2B stack wins ~60% of pistols on Anubis CT side

knowledge.title: Anubis force buy: target B site
knowledge.description: Force buys on Anubis hit B more reliably than A — the water entry creates two sightlines that punish a stacked CT side. Force with armor + Galils + flashes for water push. Avoid A force on Anubis: heaven punishes under-buys hard.
knowledge.situation: Anubis T-side force buy decision
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: B-side force success on Anubis is ~10 points higher than A-side force

knowledge.title: Anubis save discipline: AWP retention via T spawn
knowledge.description: Anubis save routes back through T spawn are short and safe. If you have an AWP and the round is lost, fall back to T spawn within 8 seconds — exit frag attempts on Anubis save rounds have one of the lowest success rates of all active duty maps.
knowledge.situation: Anubis save round, AWP retention
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Anubis save round AWP retention >75% for tier-1 teams

knowledge.title: Anubis A main: jiggle peek before commit
knowledge.description: A main has a wide entry that gives you a free jiggle peek to clear pit and palace angles before committing. Jiggle peek (tap A or D for one frame) at the corner — it's a no-risk information peek. Most Anubis A main deaths happen from blind pushes.
knowledge.situation: T pushing A main, dying to default angles
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: jiggle peeks reduce A main entry deaths by ~30%

knowledge.title: Anubis mid window duel: pre-aim for the lean
knowledge.description: Mid window duels are decided by who pre-aims the right pixel. The window opens just above head level — pre-aim slightly high. Most mid window AWP duels go to whoever already had the crosshair on the gap.
knowledge.situation: AWP mid window duel
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: top AWPers hold mid window with crosshair pre-aimed >90% of contact moments

knowledge.title: Anubis B halls: shoulder peek to bait first AWP shot
knowledge.description: Before committing to B halls, shoulder peek to bait the CT AWP. If you draw the shot, push immediately — the AWPer is in scope-out animation for ~0.4s. If no shot, you've at least confirmed there is no AWP holding the angle.
knowledge.situation: T B halls push, AWP suspected
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: shoulder peek + commit on no-shot wins ~55% of B halls AWP duels

knowledge.title: Anubis mid-round: window contact = commit to opposite site
knowledge.description: If you make contact in mid window and trade, the CT side has rotated mid players. Commit to whichever site the contact is FURTHEST from. Window contact is a hard trigger — do not slow play after a mid trade on Anubis.
knowledge.situation: Mid contact, deciding next move
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: post-mid-contact opposite-site execute wins ~62% on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis IGL: information from connector defines the call
knowledge.description: On a default round, send one player to connector for an early read on the CT setup. Connector gives you sound cues for both sites within 25 seconds. This is the highest-value information point on Anubis for an IGL deciding to commit A or B.
knowledge.situation: IGL building map information
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: connector info player is standard role on Anubis tier-1 defaults

knowledge.title: Anubis fake A: 30 second commit window
knowledge.description: An effective Anubis A fake commits utility for ~30 seconds (smokes, flashes, gunfire) then re-rotates to B. The CT will burn rotation cooldowns and B will be soft. Fakes shorter than 25 seconds do not pull rotates and waste utility.
knowledge.situation: Fake execute strategy
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: 30-second A fake to B execute success rate ~58% on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis A retake: smoke palace + flash from connector
knowledge.description: A retake: smoke palace (denies the lurk angle), flash from connector blind, push as a 3-stack. The retake difficulty on Anubis A comes from how many angles a planted T can hold — utility-driven retakes outperform aim retakes by ~20 points.
knowledge.situation: CT retake A, full retake utility
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: utility retakes on Anubis A win ~48%, aim retakes win ~28%

knowledge.title: Anubis B post-plant: hold from B halls lurk
knowledge.description: Post-plant on B, the optimal lurk is back at B halls — you can pre-fire any CT pushing through halls AND see anyone rotating from A. The platform lurk is a trap: you get traded by retake utility.
knowledge.situation: T post-plant B, choosing lurk
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: B halls post-plant outperforms platform post-plant by ~12 points on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis A plant default: behind statue, exposed to heaven
knowledge.description: Default A plant on Anubis is behind statue, exposed to heaven. This forces the CT to defuse from the most punished angle on the site. Avoid plants in connector corner — heaven cannot punish the defuse there.
knowledge.situation: T A plant location
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: behind-statue plant has highest post-plant win rate of all Anubis A plant spots

knowledge.title: Anubis anti-strat: counter B canal rush with bridge molly
knowledge.description: If the T keeps rushing B through canal, molly the canal exit before they arrive. The molly forces the rush to wait or take 50+ damage. Follow with a flash from site to counter-peek the damaged players. Canal rushes are utility-dependent — deny the utility timing.
knowledge.situation: CT defending against repeated B canal rushes on Anubis
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: canal exit molly drops B rush success rate from ~50% to ~20%

knowledge.title: Anubis anti-strat: counter A connector aggression with smoke
knowledge.description: If the T takes connector control every round, smoke connector entrance at round start and play a retake setup instead. Let them take connector — they waste time and utility in an empty area while you set up A crossfire. Deny the space they want to fight for.
knowledge.situation: CT losing connector control to T aggression on Anubis
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: giving up connector and playing retake setup succeeds ~55% on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis entry: A main entry with double flash
knowledge.description: Entry through A main on Anubis requires two flashes — one for the bridge angle and one for heaven. The first flash clears the close hold, the second covers the vertical angle. Single-flash entries leave heaven unchecked, which is the #1 entry death spot.
knowledge.situation: Entry fragger on Anubis A main push
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: double flash A main entry succeeds ~55% vs ~30% single flash on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis AWP: mid connector AWP hold
knowledge.description: The CT AWP on Anubis holds mid connector — it covers the B rotate path AND can rotate to A in 5 seconds. Connector AWP is the safest default AWP position on Anubis because it provides info on both sites.
knowledge.situation: AWP player on CT Anubis, default position
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: connector AWP on Anubis provides info on ~70% of T setups

knowledge.title: Anubis lurk: connector lurk during B execute
knowledge.description: When the team executes B, the lurker holds connector. CTs rotating from A through mid walk into the lurk. Anubis rotations through connector are fast — time the ambush for 5-7 seconds after the B execute starts.
knowledge.situation: Lurker on T-side Anubis during B execute
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: connector lurk catches A-to-B rotators ~45% of the time on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis support: B site retake utility from ruins
knowledge.description: Support on Anubis CT should save a smoke and molly for B retakes. Smoke the canal exit (cuts the lurk), molly the B plant area. This 2-utility combo isolates the T players on site and raises B retake success from ~35% to ~50%.
knowledge.situation: Support player utility for Anubis B retake
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: B retake with smoke+molly succeeds ~50% vs ~25% dry on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis clutch: 1v1 A site — use heaven angle
knowledge.description: In a 1v1 on Anubis A as T, plant behind statue and hold from heaven if possible. The heaven angle overlooks the entire A site — the CT must expose themselves to defuse. If heaven is not accessible, hold from connector angle watching the plant.
knowledge.situation: 1v1 clutch on Anubis A as T
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: heaven angle post-plant wins ~60% of 1v1 T clutches on Anubis A

knowledge.title: Anubis: T-side water control for B split
knowledge.description: Take water control early (clear the underwater passage) and use it for a B split. Water approach + canal approach simultaneously catches B defense from two angles. Water control is unique to Anubis and most CTs underestimate it.
knowledge.situation: T-side Anubis, planning a B split
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: water + canal B split has ~65% plant rate when both entries arrive within 3 seconds

knowledge.title: Anubis: CT rotate timing — A to B in 10 seconds
knowledge.description: Anubis has fast CT rotations through mid — A to B takes only 10 seconds running. This means fakes must commit more utility to be convincing. A 15-second fake may not draw rotations because CTs know they can re-rotate quickly.
knowledge.situation: T-side Anubis, fake timing considerations
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: Anubis fakes need >20 seconds of commitment to reliably draw CT rotations

knowledge.title: Anubis IGL: mid control reads for site commit
knowledge.description: At the 0:25 mark, assess mid window control. If your player holds window unchallenged, the CT is stacking sites — commit to whichever site has fewer sound cues. If window is contested hard, the CT is playing mid aggression and both sites are soft. Call a fast site hit.
knowledge.situation: T-side Anubis, IGL reading mid control for site commit
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid control reads correctly predict CT stack distribution in ~58% of rounds on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis support: A retake smoke bridge + flash from connector
knowledge.description: Save one smoke and one flash for A retakes. Smoke bridge to isolate T reinforcements from mid, then flash from connector for the push. This two-piece utility package isolates the planted T from support and raises A retake success by ~12 points.
knowledge.situation: Support player holding utility for A retake on Anubis
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: bridge smoke + connector flash A retake succeeds ~50% vs ~32% dry on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis clutch: 1v2 B site — use water angle post-plant
knowledge.description: In a 1v2 on Anubis B as T post-plant, fall back to the water connector entrance. CTs retaking from CT spawn and platform must check water last. The water angle gives sight on the bomb from cover and lets you hear both approach directions before they see you.
knowledge.situation: 1v2 clutch on Anubis B site as T
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: water angle post-plant on Anubis B wins ~25% of 1v2 T clutches

knowledge.title: Anubis AWP: B long angle from site
knowledge.description: Hold the B long sightline from the back of B site with the AWP. The long angle covers the entire canal approach and catches T entries before they reach site. After the first kill, reposition behind the pillar — the T team will flash the angle next round.
knowledge.situation: CT AWP holding B long on Anubis
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: B long AWP averages 0.7 opening kills per half on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis anti-strat: counter mid push with window molly
knowledge.description: If the T keeps pushing mid aggressively, molly the mid window area at round start. This denies the T mid control for 7 seconds and forces them to waste utility clearing the angle. Follow with a flash from connector to counter-peek any T that pushes through the fading molly.
knowledge.situation: CT losing mid to aggressive T pushes on Anubis
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: window molly delays T mid control by 7+ seconds on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis entry: B canal push with double flash
knowledge.description: Enter B through canal with two flashes — the first blinds the platform close hold, the second covers the water connector angle. Push immediately after the second flash. Single-flash entries leave the water angle unchecked, which accounts for ~35% of B entry deaths.
knowledge.situation: Entry fragger pushing B through canal on Anubis
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: double flash canal entry succeeds ~52% vs ~28% single flash on Anubis B

knowledge.title: Anubis lurk: A main lurk during B execute
knowledge.description: When the team executes B, lurk at A main entrance. CTs rotating from A through mid to B pass the A main sightline. Time the peek for 6-8 seconds after the B execute starts. The A main lurk on Anubis is underutilized — most teams lurk connector instead, making A main unexpected.
knowledge.situation: Lurker on T-side Anubis during B execute, A main position
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: A main lurk catches A-to-B rotators ~30% of the time — less frequent but higher surprise factor

knowledge.title: Anubis economy: pistol round water push
knowledge.description: On T pistol, buy P250 + flash and push through water to B. Water creates an off-angle that CT pistol setups rarely cover. Stack 4 water + 1 passive A main for a late fake. The water approach on pistol catches B defenders off guard ~55% of the time.
knowledge.situation: T pistol round water push strategy on Anubis
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: water push on T pistol wins ~52% on Anubis — above map average

knowledge.title: Anubis positioning: B defense from ruins angle
knowledge.description: Hold B from the ruins angle behind the back pillar. This position covers the canal entry and has an escape route to CT spawn. The ruins angle forces the T to clear deep before planting, buying your teammate time to rotate. Fall back after the first kill.
knowledge.situation: CT B defense positioning on Anubis
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: ruins angle B hold wins first contact ~55% on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis A retake: pillar push with smoke bridge
knowledge.description: Retake A from the pillar angle with a smoke on bridge to cut T reinforcements. Push from pillar toward site with a flash from heaven. The pillar approach avoids the common A main post-plant angles. Without the bridge smoke, T players cycle through mid for free flanks.
knowledge.situation: CT retaking A from pillar on Anubis
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: pillar retake with bridge smoke succeeds ~46% on Anubis A

knowledge.title: Anubis utility: mid smoke for safe cross
knowledge.description: Throw a mid smoke from T spawn that blocks the window sightline. This single smoke enables safe crossing from A main to B canal through mid. Without the smoke, the CT window player picks off crossers ~40% of the time. The smoke is the most cost-effective T utility on Anubis.
knowledge.situation: T-side Anubis, safe mid crossing
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: mid cross smoke reduces T crossing deaths from ~40% to ~5% on Anubis

knowledge.title: Anubis mid-round: water control after 0:45
knowledge.description: After the 0:45 mark, water control becomes the deciding factor for B commits. If you hold water, you have a flanking path to B that bypasses canal entirely. Send one player through water at 0:45 to establish the angle — late water pushes catch the B anchor repositioning.
knowledge.situation: T-side Anubis, water control for late B commit
knowledge.pro_example: Pro meta: late water control raises B execute success by ~12 points on Anubis
